sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel.

Single ungated normal space pocket. The damage can be overwhelming if you warp to the site at zero range. The battleships have 80 km range.

sections.Escalation:
Angel Domination Fleet Staging Point


Systems have intercepted and decrypted an encoded DED transmission suggesting the presence of another Angel Cartel fleet staging area nearby. The location has been uploaded to the Expedition panel in your NeoCom's Journal.

This site can escalate to Angel Domination Fleet Staging Point~ and also Angel Shielded Starbase and Angel Occupied Mine(as of March 2025)

npcTables.text: Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


The Amarr Empire has had its share of religious cults and fanatics in the past, but few have been as successful, or as highly feared, as the Blood Raiders. The Blood Raiders are a sect of an ancient cult called Sani Sabik, who use blood in their rituals. The Blood Raiders believe that cloned bodies have "purer" blood than other bodies, and this explains while they operate mainly in space, attacking unwary spacefarers and draining their bodies of blood. The Blood Raiders are led by the fearsome Omir Sarikusa, who has remained on top of the DED's most wanted list for many years now. Under Sarikusa's leadership, the Blood Raiders have become more organized, establishing several bases in the Bleak Lands region.

Single ungated normal space pocket.

sections.Walkthrough:
The site is an open deadspace pocket, warpable at any range.  It can be easily soloed in a HAC, Battleship, T3C or Battlecruiser. The site consists of an asteroid colony surrounded by snake-shaped rocks.

On warpin


The Amarr Empire has had its share of religious cults and fanatics in the past, but few have been as successful, or as highly feared, as the Blood Raiders. The Blood Raiders are a sect of an ancient cult called Sani Sabik, who use blood in their rituals. The Blood Raiders believe that cloned bodies have "purer" blood than other bodies, and this explains while they operate mainly in space, attacking unwary spacefarers and draining their bodies of blood. The Blood Raiders are led by the fearsome Omir Sarikusa, who has remained on top of the DED's most wanted list for many years now. Under Sarikusa's leadership, the Blood Raiders have become more organized, establishing several bases in the Bleak Lands region.

On warp-in


Formed by two former members of the Caldari Navy who go by the names "Fatal" and "the Rabbit," the Guristas are a constant thorn in the side of the Caldari State. Traditional pirates in the sense that their operation is not based around some creed or ideology, the Guristas are motivated by plain and simple greed. They have bases close to Caldari space, and from them they embark on daring raids, often into the State itself. Though the Guristas are considered more honorable than many of their piratical counterparts, they are still extremely dangerous and not to be trifled with.

rows: Formed by two former members of the Caldari Navy who go by the names "Fatal" and "the Rabbit," the Guristas are a constant thorn in the side of the Caldari State. Traditional pirates in the sense that their operation is not based around some creed or ideology, the Guristas are motivated by plain and simple greed. They have bases close to Caldari space, and from them they embark on daring raids, often into the State itself. Though the Guristas are considered more honorable than many of their piratical counterparts, they are still extremely dangerous and not to be trifled with.

sections.Walkthrough:
There are two variations of this combat site. The first variation takes place in the Infested Station Ruin and includes Heavy Missile Batteries in the initial wave. The second variation takes place in the Drone Structure I and includes Sentry Missile Batteries in the initial wave. Suits speed tanked missile ships - Jackdaw, Caracal Navy Issue, and thought likely doable with a Vexor.
Make use of range & microwarp to wean out cruisers then bombard remaining battleships.

Infested Station
This variant contains a prominent Infested Station Ruin



On warp in:


Rogue Drones appear to have taken over this station ruin and made it their home. Your Sensors pick up the faint signature of frozen bio matter, the remains of the station's inhabitants no doubt.

Formed by two former members of the Caldari Navy who go by the names "Fatal" and "the Rabbit," the Guristas are a constant thorn in the side of the Caldari State. Traditional pirates in the sense that their operation is not based around some creed or ideology, the Guristas are motivated by plain and simple greed. They have bases close to Caldari space, and from them they embark on daring raids, often into the State itself. Though the Guristas are considered more honorable than many of their piratical counterparts, they are still extremely dangerous and not to be trifled with.

sections.Escalation: This site can escalate to Dread Guristas Fleet Staging Point.

npcTables.text: "Formed by two former members of the Caldari Navy who go by the names "Fatal" and "the Rabbit," the Guristas are a constant thorn in the side of the Caldari State. Traditional pirates in the sense that their operation is not based around some creed or ideology, the Guristas are motivated by plain and simple greed. They have bases close to Caldari space, and from them they embark on daring raids, often into the State itself. Though the Guristas are considered more honorable than many of their piratical counterparts, they are still extremely dangerous and not to be trifled with."

npcTables.text: "Sansha's Nation was founded well over a century ago. Sansha was a Caldari tycoon who carved out a sizable piece of space for himself; there, he set out to create an utopian state. His vision and charm attracted thousands of people, and for some time the Nation flourished. But Sansha became ever more warped as his success increased. He started experiments, combining capsule technology with the human mind, creating zombie-like creatures having the cold, calculating minds of computers, yet retaining the ingenuity of humans. When this terrifying truth became public knowledge, Sansha was condemned, and special forces from each of the great empires joined forces to bring him down. His "true Sansha" servitors were decimated, scattered to the furthest reaches of known space. Remnants still remain far in the outer regions, but the once-glorious Nation has been reduced to small gangs of pirates and pillagers."

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


Sansha's Nation was founded well over a century ago. Sansha was a Caldari tycoon who carved out a sizable piece of space for himself; there, he set out to create an Utopian State. His vision and charm attracted thousands of people, and for some time the Nation flourished. But Sansha became ever more warped as his success increased. He started experiments, combining capsule technology with the human mind, creating zombie-like creatures having the cold, calculating minds of computers, yet retaining the ingenuity of humans. When this terrifying truth became public knowledge, Sansha was condemned, and special forces from each of the great empires joined forces to bring him down. His "True Sansha" servitors were decimated, scattered to the furthest reaches of known space. Remnants still remain far in the outer regions, but the once glorious Nation has been reduced to small gangs of pirates and pillagers.

npcTables.text: Sansha's Nation was founded well over a century ago. Sansha was a Caldari tycoon who carved out a sizable piece of space for himself; there, he set out to create an Utopian State. His vision and charm attracted thousands of people, and for some time the Nation flourished. But Sansha became ever more warped as his success increased. He started experiments, combining capsule technology with the human mind, creating zombie-like creatures having the cold, calculating minds of computers, yet retaining the ingenuity of humans. When this terrifying truth became public knowledge, Sansha was condemned, and special forces from each of the great empires joined forces to bring him down. His "True Sansha" servitors were decimated, scattered to the furthest reaches of known space. Remnants still remain far in the outer regions, but the once glorious Nation has been reduced to small gangs of pirates and pillagers.

npcTables.text: The Serpentis Corporation was founded a few decades ago by V. Salvador Sarpati. At first, it was engaged in hi-tech research, but with time its ties to the underworld grew, and Serpentis research stations scattered around in remote areas became notorious pirate havens. Sarpati made a deal with the Angel Cartel early on to provide protection for his stations, and this duty was eventually taken on by the Guardian Angels. Both sides have prospered enormously from this deal - Serpentis can operate in peace, and the Angel Cartel gets access to the illegal research efforts of the Serpentis conglomerate. It is strongly believed that Serpentis is the main developer and manufacturer of illegal neural boosters, especially since Sarpati's father was a renowned specialist in that field. The home of Serpentis is in the Phoenix constellation in the Fountain region.

sections.Walkthrough:
This site can be done in any drone boat, such as the Vexor, Vexor Navy Issue or the Gila. 
A quick guide: When you enter the site, turn on your modules and drop drones. Then you want to move a bit away from the warp-in, drop down your Mobile Tractor Unit, start orbiting it while you attack the rats.  
You want to kill frigates first, then cruisers, battlecruiser then battleships, BUT keep an eye on what rat triggers the next wave, and leave 1 of those for last.

npcTables.text: The Serpentis Corporation was founded a few decades ago by V. Salvador Sarpati. At first it was engaged in hi-tech research, but with time its ties to the underworld grew, and Serpentis research stations scattered around in remote areas became notorious pirate havens. Sarpati made a deal with the Angel Cartel early on to provide protection for his stations, and this duty was eventually taken on by the Guardian Angels. Both sides have prospered enormously from this deal. - Serpentis can operate in peace, and the Angel Cartel gets access to the illegal research efforts of the Serpentis conglomerate. It is strongly believed that Serpentis is the main developer and manufacturer of illegal neural boosters, especially since Sarpati's farther was a renowned specialist in that field. The home of the Serpentis is in the Phoenix constellation in the Fountain region.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin


The Serpentis Corporation was founded a few decades ago by V. Salvador Sarpati.  At first it was engaged in hi-tech neural booster research, but with the discovery of their fatal side-effects, prematurely closed. The company was re-established by Igil's adopted son, Salvador Sarpati, and with time its ties with the underworld grew and the Serpentis research stations, scattered around in remote areas, became infamous pirate havens. Sarpati made a deal with the Angel Cartel early on to provide protection for his stations and the duty was taken on by the Guardian Angels. Both sides have prospered enormously for this deal - Serpentis can operate in peace and the Angel Cartel gets access to the illegal research efforts of the Serpentis. It is strongly believed that Serpentis is the main developer and manufacturer of illegal neural boosters, especially since Sarpati's father was a renown specialist in that field. The home of Serpentis is in the Phoenix constellation in the Fountain region.

Ungated pocket with multiple waves of rats.

sections.Escalation:
This anomaly can escalate to Serpentis Narcotic Warehouses.



On warp in:


This deadspace complex was built by the Serpentis Corporation to protect and store narcotic and military supplies for their members. The complex also acts as a temporary storage area for the corporation's traffic of illicit substances. While the entrance check-in sectors of the complex are not known to be heavily guarded, certain sections house experienced security staff using top-of-the-line equipment funded by the Serpentis Corporation's income from illegal activity.
DED Threat Assessment: Unsafe (3 of 10)

sections.Walkthrough:
There are two types of Havens: rock and gas. Gas havens have enemies pre-spawned, but the enemies are much more spread out. Rock havens have the enemies grouped together, but it takes a few seconds after warping in for them to spawn. 
The site can be done in any drone boat.
sections.Type 1 - Rock Haven:
The site consists of one ungated pocket. There is a hollow rock with a stargate inside about 20 km from warp-in.
The initial defenders will take a few second to arrive and new wave spawns once all ships from previous spawn are destroyed.




In local:


A large group of pirate ships have arrived through the gate. From the looks of it, they don't appear to be too friendly.

sections.Locating & Difficulty:
The site is an open pocket that can be warped to at any range.  
Two battlecruisers can run this site without too much difficulty.
sections.Walkthrough:
Each new wave spawns when the previous one is destroyed.  All enemy waves spawn around the cluster of structures.  Waves can vary significantly in composition.  Waves 2 and 3 can spawn simultaneously.  
There are multiple variations of this site with different spawns.

Variant 1 (asteroid port)

Sentries do not need to be destroyed to trigger Wave 2.

sections.Walkthrough (silo spawn):

Sentries spawn 35-40 km away from the warp-in point.  All other ships spawn at 0 on the anomaly.

rows: The Serpentis Corporation was founded a few decades ago by V. Salvador Sarpati. At first, it was engaged in hi-tech research, but with time its ties to the underworld grew, and Serpentis research stations scattered around in remote areas became notorious pirate havens. Sarpati made a deal with the Angel Cartel early on to provide protection for his stations, and this duty was eventually taken on by the Guardian Angels. Both sides have prospered enormously from this deal - Serpentis can operate in peace, and the Angel Cartel gets access to the illegal research efforts of the Serpentis conglomerate. It is strongly believed that Serpentis is the main developer and manufacturer of illegal neural boosters, especially since Sarpati's father was a renowned specialist in that field. The home of Serpentis is in the Phoenix constellation in the Fountain region.

sections.Site:
Type 1
Type 1 consists of one ungated pocket. In the pocket, you will find 5 large structures called Magnetic Double-capped Bubble and the initial NPCs between them.
If you have a mobil tractor unit, start orbiting it. If not, you can orbit one of the structures in the pocket.
Start by killing the smallest size ships, and save the next wave trigger NPC for last.

sections.Walkthrough:
When warping into the complex


This is a den for the ones that value instant worldly pleasure above other things. It is a popular stopover for overworked Angel pirates and members of secret guilds or groups.
Captain Rogue or the "Pleasure Officer" maintains a firm control of who is let further into the complex and who is not.
DED Threat Assessment: Dangerous (5 of 10)"


Room 1


Warp in Message.


Trafficking of drugs, hallucogenics, exotic art pieces and valuables is rampant in this den of carnal pleasures. Attempts to crack down on this operation have all failed as the entertainment facilities here are frequented by prominent political individuals and celebrities. Operatives should restrict themselves to monitoring only.

Room 2


Warp in Message.


A prominent figure in the art world, Mandat Gardan, has established a fairly exotic entertainment facility here. It has gained some renown as it is the only pleasure spa so far to combine hot streams of air and cool water fountains with zero gravity and imaginative architectural solutions. It is reportedly very cheap to build and a surge in this kind of entertainment is expected to happen soon.
The wheels of your imagination start turning as your ship's weapon systems warm up.

Room 3


Warp in message.


The Angel Cartel's original advertisement for this place that it had established an "old folks home" for retired Angels. What that really meant was that wealthy pirates that wanted out of the business could buy into this resort and stay in it indefinitely by signing their investments over to the cartel. By all standards, this place is extremely well guarded.

Warning: Initial incoming damage will be very high. Clear the triple Stasis Towers as fast as possible; they will web you to a standstill. Recommend over 85% resists to Explosive damage.
Angel's Retirement Home (the structure with your loot) has 93k shield HP buffer. Unless you can permatank the rats, do not attempt to go after it first.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


The massive amount of signatures you are getting on your short range scan can only indicate one thing: a full combat fleet stationed around the deadspace pocket. The Angel Cartel would seem to be planning a large-scale military operation in the area and you should expect nothing but the heaviest resistance should you try to oppose them here.

No hostiles on warpin.

Angel Domination Fleet Staging Point 2

This site can escalate to Angel Domination Fleet Staging Point 2

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


The Angel Cartel has established a firm presence in this Deadspace Complex. They use it as a naval construction base but reports claim that they perform some testing of shield Compact inside. These rumors remain largely unconfirmed. The area is extremely dangerous and the inner pockets are off limits to everyone but the key players in the Angel Cartel.DED Threat Assessment: Critical (10 of 10).

No hostiles at warpin.

1st pocket


On warpin:


This pocket serves as a scanning and identification post for the gatekeepers, habitation for the workers in the complex and a place of commerce for businessmen who mainly deal in technology, energy or mineral resources
On earned escalations only, the first gate is unlocked, allowing ships to bypass the first room completely via immediately aligning to the gate, activating micro jump drive, and taking the gate. This only applies to earned escalations, not sites that are scanned down.

On warpin:


Previous reports have indicated this first deadspace pocket is nowhere near as dangerous to enter as the rest of them. It serves as a scanning and identification post for the gatekeepers, habitation for the workers in the complex and a place of commerce for businessmen who mainly deal in technology, energy or mineral resources.

3rd pocket


On warpin:


Earlier reports have indicated huge arrays of armor fittings guarded by automatic sentries and patrolling pilots. This deadspace pocket is equipped and staffed to service an entire fleet of ships in record time, whether it be armoring, hull modifications or other types of structural upgrades. The service is expensive, but extremely fast and reliable.

Docked and Loaded Mammoth structure has 60,000 shield HP with 1700 DPS reps.  Without a high DPS marauder or a few friends its better to skip its drop.

5th pocket


On warpin:


A pilot who entered this pocket previously managed to give out only a brief distress signal before his ship was destroyed. The revived clone remembered nothing from the incident, which indicates the destruction happened almost instantly and most likely caused too much terror for the experience to register in his mind before it was coded and transmitted.





WARNING: The Antimatter Channeler deals 12500 omni damage every 60 seconds, with an emphasis on Explosive damage.  Combined with the Stasis Towers, the sheer alpha can easily punch through a poorly tanked ship.

Stasis Towers have limited engagement range and can be destroyed safely from around 50 km. Overseer’s turrets reach out to 100 km but are subject to the same tracking limits as regular battleships so speed tanking may be helpful.  Its tank is also nothing special, 500 DPS Explosive is enough to crack its shield reps.

npcTables.text: Earlier reports have indicated huge arrays of armor fittings guarded by automatic sentries and patrolling pilots. This deadspace pocket is equipped and staffed to service an entire fleet of ships in record time, whether it be armoring, hull modifications or other types of structural upgrades. The service is expensive, but extremely fast and reliable.

rows: 5- 6 x Sentry Tower Sentry Angel III
6 x Sentry Angel Cruise Missile Battery

npcTables.text: This surprisingly small drydock dispatches ships with great efficiency. Incredibly talented and exacting, the Angel ship builders charge exorbitant prices and thus most of their customers come from the wealthier end of the pilot spectrum.

rows: 2- 3 x Sentry Angel Cruise Missile Battery

npcTables.text: A pilot who entered this pocket previously managed to give out only a brief distress signal before his ship was destroyed. The revived clone remembered nothing from the incident, which indicates the destruction happened almost instantly and most likely caused too much terror for the experience to register in his mind before it was coded and transmitted.

rows: 1 x Overseer Battleship The Antimatter Channeler

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


To the frustration of the Minmatar Republic, the Angel Cartel still enjoys a great deal of success with piracy throughout Republic space. A contributing factor to the Angel's ubiquity is their ability to repurpose small gang outposts as camps.
A Domination Grigori is sent with a small frigate wing to clear out a minor smuggler outpost. Once temporary fortifications are made, the location is further masked with a series of interference broadcasts designed to confound Republic sensors.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


This military complex belongs to the Angel Cartel, which sent a crew of their finest engineers and military personnel here to establish a mining complex. Unconfirmed DED reports state that the Cartel plans to use this complex to develop new weapons, as well as practice combat maneuvers and strategies away from prying eyes.

No hostiles at initial gate. The site offers a choice of 3 acceleration gates in the 2nd Pocket each leading to an alternate variant of the 3rd pocket and further leading to a common 4th pocket.

1st Pocket


On warpin:


The rumour is that the building of this complex was only half-completed before the Angel Cartel's military operations started. Blasted asteroids and debris from reckless dogfighting litter these deadspace pockets. Caution is advised.Your transmission decoded flickers, a locator signal seemingly emanating from a typical container inside this deadspace pocket. Other transmissions indicate that some kind of a disaster has struck and a massive explosion knocked communications offline a while ago.

npcTables.text: This military complex belongs to the Angel Cartel, which sent a crew of their finest engineers and military personnel here to establish a mining complex. Unconfirmed DED reports state that the Cartel plans to use this complex to develop new weapons, as well as practice combat maneuvers and strategies away from prying eyes.

The rumour is that the building of this complex was only half-completed before the Angel Cartel's military operations started. Blasted asteroids and debris from reckless dogfighting litter these deadspace pockets. Caution is advised.Your transmission decoded flickers, a locator signal seemingly emanating from a typical container inside this deadspace pocket. Other transmissions indicate that some kind of a disaster has struck and a massive explosion knocked communications offline a while ago.
Available data indicates that this deadspace complex pocket was supposed to contain the first deadspace Stargate. Reports indicate that the Angel Cartel was not successful.

rows: 1 x Ancient Acceleration Gate Mark II(Tactical Operations Center)
1 x Ancient Acceleration Gate Mark III(Watchpost)

npcTables.text: The Angel Cartel has established a well fortified supply depot with a lot of troops stationed in military bunkers. A scout mission revealed that the battlestation in the center is not fully operational and supposedly serves as supply management HQ and storage facilities.

rows: 1 x Commander Battleship The Stronghold General

npcTables.text: Tactical operations in the entire deadspace complex are coordinated and handled from here. The batlestation is reported to be well defended.

The Outer Perimeter Watchpost is on the outskirts of the deadspace area. Despite the protection the warp-free space offers, the Angel Cartel nonetheless monitors the area closely. A CONCORD diplomatic committee is negotiating an information-sharing deal to relieve tension.
A scouting mission spotted the so-called "underground Overseer" before warping out under heavy fire.

rows: 1 x Overseer Battleship Tritan -The Underground Overseer

npcTables.text: The innermost deadspace pocket in the Angel military complex has received a lot of expensive high quality minerals. Ready-made building blocks are steadily freighted in, but there have been no reports of what they are actually building.

rows: 1 x Overseer Battleship The Battlestation Admiral

sections.Walkthrough:
Upon warping to the site you will arrive at an acceleration gate. It's possible to do this site in a ratting Ishtar but may take 3 hours to do in that manner.



Database Information


The Blood Raider Covenant is infamous for its organized strike capabilities. It is no surprise, then, that Blood Raider coordination centers are a common encounter for capsuleer and DED forces in nullsec. Numerous battle reports support the theory that each complex serves as a primary base for a Dark Blood Hunter, a specialist in tracking, subduing, and harvesting the biomass of capsuleers.
Because of the Blood Hunter's influence, the threat to civilian and Imperial assets posed by such coordination centers has been on the decline. The DED is more than willing to leave the Blood Hunters as a capsuleers' problem.
DED Threat Assessment: 7 of 10

5th pocket
You land ~60km from the tower and it webs you at that range. It has low health, but when you're webbed you're going to be taking a lot of damage. In an armour ratting Ishtar, I used warrior drones to kill the tower, due to their speed, and overheated tank and prop during this time, as well as aligning out. I had a DCU2 fitted rather than an ADC, though. I believe that the site despawns a certain amount of time after you kill the Dark Blood Hunter, but you may not have enough time to kill that guy if you leave the tower up.

sections.Walkthrough:
The Complex consists of three rooms. All these rooms have serious numbers of battleships, cruisers and frigates inside them. The third room contains an overseer structure, which when destroyed has a chance of dropping Corpus X-Type or Dark Blood modules and a Bhaalgorn BPC. The structure also always drops 23rd Tier Overseer's Personal Effects.
The complex contains many normal battleship NPCs Renamed as "Dark Corpus", however they are not faction and drop nothing of value.

sections.Walkthrough:
Anybody who want to use the gate and complete the site need Leadership V himself, which excludes alpha characters.


1st pocket


On warp in:


This is the first guarded deadspace pocket of a total of 5 reported in this complex.
Commander Chelm Soran's top enforcers organize deadspace security from the command HQ.


True Power' Assembly Security HQ has very strong tank and does not need to be killed. Should be within 30 KM in order to effectively kill it.

5th pocket


On warp in:


Reports claim this deadspace pocket houses one of True Power's most prized possessions - The "Station Ultima" Research & Development Facilities.
What they are working on is still very much a secret, but in any case it is rumored that True Power's CEO, Vizan Ankonin, himself occasionally visits the facilities to oversee production.


Station Ultima fires EM citadel torpedoes that hit extremely hard but does not have very significant tank. 60k shield HP with 0 EM resist, 300 HP/s active shield reps. 10k armor and 50k structure. As long as you immediately begin moving quickly upon landing in the pocket its damage is survivable. Theoretically it deals upwards of 500,000 EM damage with each shot but the torpedoes have capital-ship sized damage application (10,000m), and they are fired extremely slowly (every 120 seconds).

On warpin:


This deadspace complex bestows a heavily guarded prison facility of the Angel Cartel. It is here that kidnapping victims and enemies of the cartel are held for ransom or brutal interrogation and torture.

No Hostiles at initial gate.

This first pocket within the deadspace field contains the main gates to this Angel Cartel prison complex, defended by a pair of experienced frigate pilots.

Fourth room



On warpin:


This deepest section of the complex houses the prisoner block. Fully outfitted with a bloodsport arena, the Angel Cartel Prison Guards pitch their unsuspecting captives into brutal Darwinian games where only the strongest and fittest survive to tell the tale. The block is guarded by an experienced combat fleet, not to be taken lightly.
Important: The main structure (Angel Retention Facility) will steadily repair its shields, and if anything (ships or drones) is within 35~38km of it, it will web you and repair all of its armor every few seconds. This dramatically increases the DPS needed to break it.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp-in


The massive amounts of signatures you are getting on your short range scan can only indicate one thing, a full combat fleet in station around the deadspace pocket. The Guristas Pirates would seem to be planning a large scale military operation in the area and you should expect nothing but the heaviest resistance should you try to oppose them here.
DED Threat Assessment: Extreme (9 of 10)


This site is fairly hard due to large number of ships and neuting towers. It can be done in a group of T2, T3 or Faction cruisers or a few battleships.
Warping to the site lands you at 0 on an acceleration gate.

sections.Walkthrough:
Warpin message:


The existence of Gurista military complexes contradicts the common perception of the organization as romantic anarchists or modern buccaneers. Time and again both State and CONCORD authorities have encountered sprawling Gurista fortresses that display a degree of organization and manpower generally found only in effective military organizations. All such sites should be dealt with severely and their command structure broken. We do not want a repeat of Kuvakei.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


According to reliable sources within the Amarr Navy, this deadspace complex is currently occupied by a large contingency of Crimson Hand cultists. Intelligence indicates that the Crimson Hand, a militant splinter group of the Bloodraider Covenant, is currently engaged in a massive military buildup. While their continued alliance with Omir Sarikusa is in question, they do seem to be planning something big.
DED Threat Assessment: Serious (6 of 10)


Warping to the site lands on an acceleration gate at 0.

Room 1


On warp in:


Gate 1
This is a waiting area for trusted black market traders that come here to trade goods (mostly slaves and weaponry) with the Crimson Hand. Security in the area is fairly light, since this is not really a part of the base proper. Caution is still advised, since the area is usually crawling with lowlife scum from every corner of the galaxy.


Rats in this room can be from Serpentis and Angel factions along with Blood Raiders. The acceleration gate to second room is 70 km away. Sentries do not need to be destroyed to proceed.

Central Processing


On warp in:


Central Processing:
The center point of the Crimson Hand depot, this area provides protection and access control to the more sensitive supply areas of the complex. Visitors are not allowed past this point unless they have been granted clearance by a senior officer. No exceptions will be tolerated.
Warning!!
Be advised. Industrial sector has been compromised. Sentinel personnel, report to battle stations. Battle group Alpha and Beta, prepare to engage hostiles. Other battle groups stand by for further orders.










WD

EWAR

L




  
 

  1 x Sentry  Crimson Sentinel  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  12 x Battleship  Crimson Paladin/Elder  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 



Crimson Sentinel has strong active shield tank (240 HP/s), high structure hitpoints (100k), and webs to 50 km.  Save it for last, even with massive DPS it takes a long time to kill. It shoots battleship-grade Mjolnir torpedoes that deal 250 DPS EM with 1500 alpha - low enough to be able to speed tank.
Rats spawn 50-60 km from landing.  There are two acceleration gates 48 km from landing leading to the last two rooms of the complex. The Sentinel may drop Dark Blood faction modules as well as a key (Crimson Hand Level 3 Passcard) which is not required for both gates.
BSs are all tier 1 battleships (300-400k bounty , not such a threat) they have a little hp and deal relatively low damage.

This area is the primary supply sector for Crimson Hand battle groups in this system. Incoming personnel should report to their appropriate supply stations. Personnel assigned to the Command sector should go to the Administrative Offices on level 34 of the Supply Tower.
Red Alert!!!
Sentinel has fallen. I repeat, sentinel has fallen. All battle groups prepare to engage the enemy. Protect the supply rig at all cost. Hostiles must not gain access to the Administrative sector.

Sentinel personnel, report to battle stations. Battle group Alpha and Beta, prepare to engage hostiles. Other battle groups stand by for further orders.

This area is the primary supply sector for Crimson Hand battle groups in this system. Incoming personnel should report to their appropriate supply stations. Personnel assigned to the Command sector should go to the Administrative Offices on level 34 of the Supply Tower. Red Alert!!! Sentinel has fallen. I repeat, sentinel has fallen. All battle groups prepare to engage the enemy. Protect the supply rig at all cost. Hostiles must not gain access to the Administrative sector.

rows: 14 x Sentry Sentry Gun (only shoot 30 km)
8 x Sentry Blood Cruise Missile Batteries

sections.Walkthrough:
1st pocket


Database information:


This entrance pocket of the complex serves as a checkpoint to alert the internal pockets of the complex in the case of any intrusion or a Concord raid.
The acceleration gate leading towards the Recuperation Tower is scrambled by the resident Guristas pilots, making it critical to take out those peripheral defenses before advancing forth.


The acceleration gate is locked until all defenders are dead.








WD

EWAR

L

2nd pocket


Database information:


The so called 'Recuperation Tower' pocket appears to be a sector where Pith Guristas soldiers can rest and help themselves to a drink at the local cantina. It serves as a meeting point for troops coming in with weekend passes, to continue further into the festivities that take place deeper into the complex.

The acceleration gate is locked until all defenders are dead.

3rd pocket


Database information:


According to intelligence reports, this pocket has been named 'Sin Boulevard' by the mercenaries that frequent this place of degeneracy. It is the central section of the complex and contains the main attraction for visiting members of the Guristas army. The acceleration gate leading deeper into the complex is for high-rollers only, being a hub for gambling and bloodsport events.

The acceleration gate is locked until all defenders are dead.

Database information:


The final sector of the reinvigoration camp houses casinos and a large arena for pitching involuntary captives of the Pith Guristas against each other in violent bloodsport events. According to intelligence reports this pocket contains the greatest military might within the complex, being such a strong attraction for the wealthy and world-weary officers of the mercenary league.

There are total of three cruisers. Some of which jam.
The good stuff drops from Captive Fighting Arena.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


This small deadspace complex was built by the militant Guristas pirates. It serves as a scout outpost and a minor rendezvous point for reconnaissance teams.
DED Threat Assessment: Risky (4 of 10)


Tier 3 cruisers cannot enter the site.

First pocket


On warp in:


The scout base perimeter is defended by light spacecraft acting as security for the sensitive recon equipment deeper inside the complex. The security acceleration gate is known to be actively blocked by the security team, allowing no intruders entry while they remain at work.

Second pocket


On warp in:


This section of the complex houses the delicate sensor equipment used to support the Guristas coded communication network. Relay pylons and scout bases surround a small supply depot in the pocket's center, known to contain military supplies and equipment for the reconnaissance arm of the Guristas organization.

Do not underestimate the incoming damage; a T1 battlecruiser will implode under the DPS within a minute or two, although a buffer-fit Drake can clear it without dropping below 80% shields. 
Recommended to increase Kinetic resistance with two Kinetic Shield Hardeners and to also equip 10MN Afterburner for cruiser and battlecruiser fits. 
If you want to lower the damage output, it is highly recommended to destroy the cruisers, as they make up most of the incoming damage.

These storage areas and warehouses are absolutely full of hallucinogens.  It looks like there's a thick layer of additional security between the receiving area and the production facility.

Significant amount of (mostly) kinetic damage in this room. Prioritize battlecruisers and battleships first since their missiles make up most of the incoming damage and have long range.

There's the culprit, a Gurista Distributor. It's going to take a long time for these filthy pirates to replace such an integral part of their organization.

Kill Guristas Distributor then either loot his wreck or bookmark it, warp out, and come back a few minutes later to pick up the loot. Triple Stasis Tower spawn will reduce your speed to <10 m/s, making you take a LOT of damage, especially if you rely on speed-tanking.

sections.Walkthrough:
Expedition start


The Mul-Zatah Monastic order has for a long time maintained close links with Omir Sarikusa and the Blood Raider Covenant. The order is considered by most law enforcement agents to pose a limited threat to security, since they seem to keep to themselves for the most part. They are, however, notoriously intolerant of trespassers near their monasteries and it is not advisable to approach lightly armed.
DED Threat Assessment: Risky (4 of 10)

The enemies in each room remain passive until the player takes aggressive action.  Therefore, sniper-fit ships can easily and safely move to long range before starting combat, and can kill enemies well before the enemies get in range to do damage.
Short-range combat ships will face a lot of enemy cap neutralizers, so a fit that requires low or no cap to operate is recommended.

Room #1


Warp-in message


The gates of the Mul-Zatah are fiercely guarded by a small contingency of monastic knights.

Room #2


Warp-in message


Limited information is available on the Inner Sanctum of the Mul-Zatath monasteries;  and most of what is known is based on unreliable information from members of the order that have been captured in DED raids on Blood Raider strongholds. According to these cultists the Mul-Zatath engage in gruesome genetic research using Minmatar slaves legally purchased within the Amarr empire itself. The Minmatar Republic has repeatedly condemned this trade but so far with limited success.





WARNING: Long-range weaponry that reaches out to around 60 km is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. 6 Stasis Towers effectively nullify ship velocity to almost nothing. If all ships and sentries are in range, incoming damage can easily exceed 500 DPS, enough to shred a battlecruiser to pieces within a minute.

No auto-aggro on warp-in. Cruiser group will primary medium and heavy drones frequently.

sections.Walkthrough:
First Pocket



On warp in:


This outer section of the complex shows stark signs of the rogue drone infestation. The few human station structures that used to occupy this pocket have been reduced to debris, save for a single storage facility. Odd drone-built constructions dominate the scene, serving as maintenance platforms for the resident automated machinery. Scouts have reported the acceleration gate being actively scrambled by the local defense force, requiring total elimination of drones before it is possible to continue deeper into the complex.

Second Pocket



On warp in:


This pocket appears to serve as the main defense gauntlet into the complex. The primary acceleration gate is locked by an encrypted passcode, normally stored by one of the resident master drones. Another gate lies at the pocket's border, this one with a serious plasma leak that renders it all but useless for anyone not knowledgeable in the field of plasma physics. It has been speculated that this other gate may provide a shortcut to another pocket deep inside the sprawling complex.

Third Pocket

Via Ancient Acceleration Gate


On warp in:


This section used to be a small navy base specializing in plasma weapon research. The station's torn superstructure still floats around the swarming new inhabitants, emitting hazardous plasma leaks. The pocket's acceleration gate is frequently scrambled by the local drone force.Appendix:Old reports from the time the complex is thought to have been overtaken by the rogue drone strain say that at the time an infamous band of pirates were seen docking at the station, loading a secret cargo into their industrial ships. Some have speculated that the pirates summoned the drones here, intending to wipe out the complex after they had stolen all of its valuable equipment. Whether that's truly the case remains uncertain, but what is certain is that a few derelict industrial ships remain within the pocket, cluttered with the frozen bodies of known criminals.

sections.Walkthrough:
First Pocket



On warp in:


This outer section of the complex shows stark signs of the rogue drone infestation. The few human station structures that used to occupy this pocket have been reduced to debris, safe for a single storage facility. Odd drone-built constructions dominate the scene, serving as maintenance platforms for the resident automated machinery. Scouts have reported the acceleration gate being actively scrambled by the local defense force, requiring total elimination of drones before it is possible to continue deeper into the complex.

Second Pocket



On warp in:


This pocket appears to serve as the main defense gauntlet into the complex. The primary acceleration gate is locked by an encrypted passcode, normally stored by one of the resident master drones. Another gate lies at the pocket's border, this one with a serious plasma leak that renders it all but useless for anyone not knowledgeable in the field of plasma physics. It has been speculated that this other gate may provide a shortcut to another pocket deep inside the sprawling complex.

The room contains two acceleration gates. Ancient acceleration gate will take you to the third room while the surging acceleration gate will take you straight to fourth room but requires Plasma Physics IV to use. It also requires Zbikoki's Hacker Card (consumed on use) found in The Right Hand of Zazzmatazz. In a fleet only one member needs to have the hacker card. Once opened the gate will stay open for 10 seconds an allow passage to everyone with the required skill without requiring new hacker card.
The Surging Acceleration Gate can be used to blitz the site by skipping the fourth and fifth pockets, but you will miss out on some of the site's loot. If you want the full loot from the site, take the Ancient Acceleration Gate.

Some have speculated that the pirates summoned the drones here, intending to wipe out the complex after they had stolen all of its valuable equipment. sections.Walkthrough:
On Warp-in


Pith Penal Complexes are run by the retired Amarr captain Dewak Humfry, a fiercely disciplined tactician often referred to as 'Screaming' Dewak by his prisoners. After his service in the Imperial Navy Dewak decided to go into the prison business in the private sector, and business is clearly booming.
DED Threat Assessment: Perilous (8 of 10)


The First Gate Required CPU Management 5

1st pocket
Enemies have special names. "Assistant Warden" battleships have ECM. Rats are simply renamed; they don't drop anything atypical from the usual loot.

2nd pocket


On warp-in


This corridor is used to guard prisoners that are deemed incapable of breaking out by themself (and thus safe enough to contain in low-security facilities) but are "connected" well enough to demand a high security deadspace pocket.
Prisoners, brought here for rehabilitation, have their equipment confiscated by the authorities. Then it is "taxed" heavily to pay upkeep costs and it often leaves the prisoners without any belongings. Dewak keeps these taxes under lock and key, guarded by his number-one warden.


The ships are spread out in 2 groups.

rows: Pith Penal Complexes are run by the retired Amarr captain Dewak Humfry, a fiercely disciplined tactician often referred to as 'Screaming' Dewak by his prisoners. After his service in the Imperial Navy Dewak decided to go into the prison business in the private sector, and business is clearly booming. DED Threat Assessment: Perilous (8 of 10)
4 x Sentry Guristas Heavy Missile Battery

This corridor is used to guard prisoners that are deemed incapable of breaking out by themself (and thus safe enough to contain in low-security facilities) but are "connected" well enough to demand a high security deadspace pocket. Prisoners, brought here for rehabilitation, have their equipment confiscated by the authorities. Then it is "taxed" heavily to pay upkeep costs and it often leaves the prisoners without any belongings. Dewak keeps these taxes under lock and key, guarded by his number-one warden.
4 x Sentry Guristas Heavy Missile Battery
1 x Overseer Battleship Screaming Dewak Humfry

sections.Walkthrough:
Upon warping to the gate


This small outpost acts as a monitoring and coordination hub for the Centus division of the Sansha armed forces in this sector. It also relays communications between neighboring battletowers.
DED Threat Assessment: Unsafe (3 of 10)


Room 1






WD

EWAR

L




  
 

  2 x Sentry  Tower Sentry Sansha I  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  8 x Frigate  Centus Black Ops Agent (Centii Enslaver)  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  2 x Frigate  Centus Black Ops Agent (Centii Plague)  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  3 x Destroyer  Centus Black Ops Assassin (Centior Cannibal)  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  2 x Destroyer  Centus Black Ops Assassin (Centior Misshape)  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  1 x Overseer Frigate  Sansha Black Ops Squad Leader  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 



The Squad Leader has much stronger resists and is immune to stasis webifiers, target painters, and possibly other EWAR. The Squad Leader drops a Command Relay Key which is needed to open the next gate and is reusable. All ships must be destroyed to unlock the next gate, in addition to the key. Note that the key is not used up by the gate.

Room 2


Upon taking the gate


Relay systems such as this one are the heart and soul of Sansha's Nation.  Broadcasting a constant stream of strange and incomprehensible directives from a long-dead master.  Even so, the Centus squadron guarding this base should not be dismissed as mere mindless drones.

The damage in this room can exceed 600DPS if you are not careful; however, the room is mostly destroyers, and they only deal significant damage within 10km. The destroyers are also fairly squishy, so they die quickly, reducing room DPS. 
If you are in a well-fit (t2 or equivalent) cruiser, you can safely ignore everything except the Centus Black Ops Commander. Quickly kill him, burn to his wreck, loot, and warp off. Only one heavy missile battery will aggro on warp-in, so the sentries will not be a major concern as long as you keep your distance.

As a T1 cruiser, you can launch a single light drone, make it shoot once in an overseer frigate, and scoop it back. This will trigger his aggro and lure him out of the group.

The Centus Black Ops Commander has high omni resists in shield(90% to all damage types) but is weak in armor and structure.

EWAR

L

Upon destroying the Centus Black Ops Commander, if the site was from an escalation:


The Black Ops Commander has been destroyed, and with that, this space is safe from Sansha's mindless slaves once more.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


This complex was once the work field of a wealthy mining corporation. Funding got cut shortly after an architectural mishap, where the acceleration gates that were supposed to carry the massive industrial haulers got infected with a mysterious virus. The gates' power coils were severely drained and are to this day only capable of hurling smaller vessels between complex pockets. The ruins are now reputed to be infested with a strain of rogue drones.

First Pocket

First Pocket
This pocket contains 1 Omber (about 18k units) and 11 Veldspar asteroids.
A harvester fleet can spawn in this pocket. Maximum size of the fleet is unknown, but can contain at least two with Serpentis Harvesters and a Serpentis defense ship. 
The Harvester wrecks can drop an Elara Restrained Mining Laser Upgrade module, as well as minerals.


Asteroids

EWAR

  1 x  Omber

Second Pocket

This pocket contains 2 Omber (only containing about 500 units total) and 28 Veldspar asteroids.



On warp in:


This section of the complex was once a pocket of very valuable minerals. Most of the ore was mined away decades ago by the resident mining corporation, but the drifting hulks of rock are now kept company by another type of scavenger: rogue drones.

Third Pocket

Third Pocket
This pocket contains 8 Omber (about 93k units) and 9 Veldspar asteroids.



On warp in:


This complex pocket once housed a thriving center for miners operating in the deadspace field. The old station ruins that still remain within the sector are evidence enough that some decades back, this area was highly valued by whatever mining interest controlled it.
Multiple signals and reports of rogue drone activity have been detected around this central area. It appears these abandoned ruins have now become the home of a hostile strain of rogue drones who will stop at nothing to defend their harvesting operation.

sections.Mining:
The site's signature will vanish once the last few rogue drones in the last pocket are defeated. Afterwards, leaving the site will result in the site despawning after a minute or so unless someone is present in the site. The site can very well despawn as you're warping back to it.
The first pocket has a sizable amount of Omber in one asteroid, the second pocket has two asteroids with low amounts and the final pocket has a mixed amount of high and low amounts of Omber in each asteroid.
If the site is present in Caldari space, it is recommended to use the Endurance or Venture to be able to defeat ECM rats with drones that can sporadically appear. The Endurance also boasts a larger mining hold to minimize ore drop-offs. In other empires, ECM is not a problem and the Prospect may be used as it can tank the rats through signature tanking and active tanking modules. The gate appears to accommodate Cruiser-sized ships, but mining barges and porpoises will not fit.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


...Sansha's Nation continues its raids against poorly defended civilian areas. Mining colonies, merchant waypoints, and research outposts are favorite targets. Once the structures are overrun by Sansha slaves and drones, surviving locals are turned into test subjects. Many of these areas are used to develop new neural paralytic agents used in abductions. A common thread in numerous reports is the presence of a Sansha known as Ation09....
DED Threat Assessment: 5 of 10


Room 1
Full room aggro immediately after warp-in. You need to clear the entire pocket (minus the one sentry) to unlock the acceleration gate, which is ~45 km away.







WD

EWAR

L




  
 

  1 x Sentry  Tower Sentry Sansha II  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  20 x Frigate  Centii Butcher/Manslayer  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  5 x Cruiser  Centum Beast/Hellhound  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  2 x Battlecruiser  Centatis Phantasm  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 





  
 

  3 x Battleship  Centus Savage/Beast/Plague Lord  
 

  
 

    
 

   
 

 



Room 2


On warp in:


The Sansha have completely covered this abandoned mining outpost with sentry guns. They're quite serious about defending whatever is in the next area.

The majority of the incoming DPS in this pocket comes from the battlecruisers and battleships, destroying them significantly reduces incoming damage, which can exceed 700 DPS.
Alternatively, you can keep the battleships at around 60-70 km and the battlecruisers at more than 40 km, the DPS is significantly reduced.  Long-range weapons are recommended for this method.  Smaller ships can simply destroy the cruiser and battlecruiser spawns, as the battleships' tracking is poor enough for a cruiser to speed-tank relatively easily.
Unlike other DED sites of this rating, the gate in this pocket is unlocked from the beginning. If your ship is fast and can field a strong tank of upwards of ~700 DPS EM/Thermal, you can simply cross the 40 km distance to the gate and skip the pocket entirely.

Room 3


Transmission from Slave Ation09:


When you wake up in your next clone, remember it was the Master's will that you died here today.

This pocket is the single most dangerous in the site.  The triple stasis tower spawn will web you down to a standstill and make you an easy target for even the battleships to hit, even in fast moving cruisers.  Additionally the weapons range of most of the battleships is very large and the smaller ships will close in quickly. Killing the stasis towers should be your highest priority, unless you can field at least 500 DPS/tank with over 80% EM/Thermal resists, in which then you can opt to immediately snipe the overseer. Once the towers die the rest of the fleet is significantly easier to speed-tank.
Slave Ation09 is located around 40-45 km from the warp-in.

npcTables.text: Despite the resource drain of their recent incursions throughout traveled space, Sansha’s Nation has maintained a military presence in deadspace pockets. One favored Sansha strategy is to hide outposts behind isolated or abandoned Amarr stations. All Sansha communications are then encrypted with an adaptive security key and hidden in pre-recorded Amarr chatter. The complex security is dependent upon the True Sansha Archduke, a vessel containing a physical network of True Slaves that serve as a living security key for their network. Eliminating the Archduke will render all Nation assets inoperable. DED Threat Assessment: 7 of 10

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


The Serpentis are doing quite well for themselves in the narcotics department. Trafficking sites are bustling with activity and security.
Security forces should be mindful of any Sarpati family enforcers.
DED Threat Assessment: 5 of 10.


The damage can be overwhelming. However, in a cruiser sized ship, with a 100mn afterburner, you should be able to avoid most damage by orbiting a stationary target at around ~25km at ~900m/sec. Just make sure you target the rats which can hit you first, there should only be about 3-5 per pocket which can. However, this method becomes a bit difficult to execute due to the webs in the last room, so be wary. The webs practically make you a stationary target for the rats to shoot. 
The names and amount of ships listed may vary slightly.
If you want to blitz the last pocket, head-shot the Sarpati Family Enforcer, bookmark their wreck, and warp out. Then come back by warping to the bookmark, since the gates will already have despawned, as will have all the remaining rats in the final room, and you can scoop the loot.

sections.3rd Pocket:
The initial damage is not high since most of the larger ships have limited damage projection, however due to the triple Stasis Tower webbing, they will close in quickly and apply extreme damage if the stasis towers aren't primaried. Alternatively, if you can project at least 500 DPS out to ~40-50 km, you can opt to immediately kill the Overseer, bookmark his wreck and come back to pick up the loot when the site despawns.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


Sansha's War Supply Complex was built as a direct countermeasure to CONCORD's increased emphasis on curbing the influence of Sansha's Nation. The compound contains a lot of basic armaments and supplies. It is sturdy and well defended.
DED Threat Assessment: Serious (6 of 10)


Warping to the site lands on an acceleration gate at 0.

Room 1


On warp in:


The Check-in Decks of Sansha's War Supply are guarded by a tight group of pirate vessels. Some firepower is required to bash through to the gates.

The overseer battleship does not contain any notable loot.

Room 2a - Supply Pit


On warp in:


Here, Sansha's 'True Creations' store some of the products manufactured in the complex. A tightly built city called simply "The Pit" is heavily guarded by a number of small frigates and cruisers.
DED experts have described the area's patrol routines as flawed and their command as panicky and incompetent.

Room 2b (Product Park)


On warp-in


The so called "Product Park" is where the complex's most vigorous mining operations take place.
Structures built into asteroids house the pre-processing and manufacture of crude parts before being shipped further into the complex.


The rats are spread out in 4 groups. Except for the group camping the warp-in, they will not auto-aggro on landing unless approached or attacked, and will shoot drones if they have not aggro'd any players already.

Room 3


On warp-in


The Centus Creations station is built in the remnants of a massive asteroid. It is where the Centus Sansha mass-produce the largest components of ships and other equipment.

Group 1 auto-aggros on landing. Group 2 will not aggro unless approached or attacked and will shoot drones if not aggressed first by a player.

Room 4


On warp-in


From here, Asunula Aarikken, True Creations' assembly manager, spearheads the mass production of war supplies for Sansha's Nation.
Modified Solar Harvesters harvest natural deadspace-energy storms and channel the energy they collect towards a high-tech and luxurious manufacturing station.


The two sentries and the group camping the warp-in will auto-aggro on landing. Attacking Skomener Effotber will aggro the entire pocket.
Skomener Effotber has strong armor tank that needs at least 600 DPS of pure EM damage (70% weakest resist) to break. He does ~450 dps EM/Thermal damage at 70km range. Combined with the full room aggro the total incoming damage in this pocket can well exceed 1000 dps. There are no webbing ships so speed tanking will reduce the room damage by a lot.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warp in:


This is an A-Class repair and resupply post, operated by the Serpentis. Not only is it important to their operations, but has also been rumoured to be one of many hideouts utilized by their resistance movement. They are supposedly planning a revolt against the vicious oppression of the shadowy ruling body, the Furies, and will defend themselves and their compatriots with vicious resistance if attacked.

npcTables.text: This is an A-Class repair and resupply post, operated by the Serpentis. Not only is it important to their operations, but has also been rumoured to be one of many hideouts utilized by their resistance movement. They are supposedly planning a revolt against the vicious oppression of the shadowy ruling body, the Furies, and will defend themselves and their compatriots with vicious resistance if attacked.

Message on warpin:


This deadspace complex is the fleet shipyard headquarters of the Core department of the Serpentis Corporation. It is defended by a massive battle fleet, manufactured by the corporation and assisted by the best mercenaries available.
DED Threat Assessment: Critical (10 of 10)


Warping to the site lands on an acceleration gate at 0.

Room 1


Upon entry


The entrance pocket of this Serpentis Fleet Shipyard complex serves as a checkpoint for clearance of authorized personnel and pedestrian traffic, intended to enforce circulation control measures and other regulations. This sector is lightly defended compared to the battle fleet that resides deeper within the complex.

Serpentis Stronghold shoots Citadel Thermal Torpedos and webs within 50 km.
If all player ships are greater than 50 km from the Serpentis Stronghold, it won't repair its armor damage.  It still repairs full shields every few seconds, but it can be killed more easily this way.
There is a glitch where Serpentis Stronghold will stop repair its armor damage if it switches target.

sections.Walkthrough:
The Serpentis Narcotic warehouse consists of total of five deadspace pockets. Due to branched nature only four of them need to be entered to get the final reward.

First Pocket

First Pocket


On warp in:


The entrance pocket of this complex contains light peripheral security around the advent ware storages. Two acceleration gates give way to two different routes inside the complex, one leading to the check-in tunnel and the other to the more heavily defended security corridor. Visitors are cleared by security at the Traffic Management office, allowing members of the Serpentis Corporation access to the Check-in Tunnel.

Fastest way through this room is to jump into security corridor ignoring all the rats in the room.

Fourth Pocket - Administration Sector

Fourth Pocket


On warp in:


The final sector contains the Supply Stronghold which houses the administration staff for the entire complex. No further information is available.

The Serpentis Supply Stronghold has a lot of hit points, so it is recommended to bring a high DPS ship. It does not, however, repair itself. Shooting it will aggro all ships.

sections.Loot:
The main attraction of this site is the Coreli A-Type modules. The Small Armor Repairer and Small Remote Armor Repairer aren't as strong as corresponding deadspace modules from other factions due to widespread farming and cheaper prices. The Multispectrum Coating and prop mods are just as strong as corresponding deadspace modules from other factions.
It is also possible to get faction modules from the overseers but this is very rare.

npcTables.text: This deadspace complex was built by the Serpentis Corporation to protect and store narcotic and military supplies for their members. The complex also acts as a temporary storage area for the corporation's traffic of illicit substances. While the entrance check-in sectors of the complex are not known to be heavily guarded, certain sections house experienced security staff using top-of-the-line equipment funded by the Serpentis Corporation's income from illegal activity. DED Threat Assessment: Unsafe (3 of 10)

The entrance pocket of this complex contains light peripheral security around the advent ware storages. Two acceleration gates give way to two different routes inside the complex, one leading to the check-in tunnel and the other to the more heavily defended security corridor. Visitors are cleared by security at the Traffic Management office, allowing members of the Serpentis Corporation access to the Check-in Tunnel.

rows: 1 x Acceleration Gate to Security Corridor
1 x Acceleration Gate to Check-In Tunnel

npcTables.text: Check-in tunnel contains warehouses and storage's for equipment and drugs ready for categorization and registration. Incoming vessels are required to check in at the Staff Quarters before continuing to the main drug refinery and central warehouses.

rows: 1 x Acceleration Gate to Central Warehouses

npcTables.text: The security corridor is the main defense barrier before continuing through the acceleration gate towards the main refinery and central warehouses. All guest passes and operation handling is made by the Chief of Security on duty.

On Warp In:


Though considered little more than Angel Cartel lackeys, in recent years the Serpentis have built up their own paramilitary force, far from Curse, who specialize in guerilla strikes. Their strategy is to harass Federation and CONCORD forces so that Serpentis booster manufacturing programs remain a low-priority target. In general this strategy has worked very well. Eliminating the leadership of these Serpentis guerilla teams is a top priority for any security forces. DED Threat Assessment: 7 of 10.

sections.Walkthrough:
On Warp In:


"The Serpentis Corporation has long been the principal source for boosters and other designer drugs traded in open space. One of the keys to their continued success is a strict internal policy of monitoring and controlling the drug use of their own employees. Members of Serpentis Corp deemed too addicted to their own products are sent to Serpentis prison facilities. Here the inmates are subjected to extreme detoxing methods, from complete isolation to gene-alteration procedures.
Rumors persist of Serpentis using the more “creative” of their inmates in extreme R and D programs, essentially working them to death in pursuit of better starship modules.
DED Threat Assessment: 8 of 10"

Room 4


On Warp In:


Approaching the Administrative Complex: The panicked comms I am reading indicate that an administration facility on the other side of this gate houses a stockpile of experimental Serpentis tech.My best guess is the facility will be the one that shoots back.

Note: The Serpentis Prisoner Isolation Facility (the structure that contains your loot) has a very strong web and will immediately target you upon warp in. Be prepared to not be able to speed tank or move closer to the structure. Also of note is that it shoots XL Inferno torpedoes that do approx 2000 thermal damage, though their explosion radius is appropriate to the signature radius of capital ships so they apply little damage to battleships or smaller ships. The outpost also repairs itself to the tune of about 1000 DPS and has lots of structure hit points. 
Incoming damage can be very high by reason of the Stasis Towers and the structure's web. Suggest refitting for maximum tank. Kill the webbing towers first, then the Elite Frigates, then move on to the rest. Profile is shown here: Database Entry 

There is a glitch [Note 1] on this structure whereby the structure will stop repairing armor if it switches aggro. The structure's armor repair can be turned off by forcing it to switch its primary target (the target it fires torpedoes at and webs). A drone ship does this by having its drones out and attacking the structure, then flying over 120km away from it. Players in ships without drones will have to have ensure another pilot is in space within application range of the structure.

sections.Room 4:
Note: The Serpentis Prisoner Isolation Facility (the structure that contains your loot) has a very strong web and will immediately target you upon warp in. Be prepared to not be able to speed tank or move closer to the structure. Also of note is that it shoots XL Inferno torpedoes that do approx 2000 thermal damage, though their explosion radius is appropriate to the signature radius of capital ships so they apply little damage to battleships or smaller ships. The outpost also repairs itself to the tune of about 1000 DPS and has lots of structure hit points.
Incoming damage can be very high by reason of the Stasis Towers and the structure's web. Suggest refitting for maximum tank. Kill the webbing towers first, then the Elite Frigates, then move on to the rest. Profile is shown here: Database Entry
There is a glitch [Note 1] on this structure whereby the structure will stop repairing armor if it switches aggro. The structure's armor repair can be turned off by forcing it to switch its primary target (the target it fires torpedoes at and webs). A drone ship does this by having its drones out and attacking the structure, then flying over 120km away from it. Players in ships without drones will have to have ensure another pilot is in space within application range of the structure.

npcTables.text: "The Serpentis Corporation has long been the principal source for boosters and other designer drugs traded in open space. One of the keys to their continued success is a strict internal policy of monitoring and controlling the drug use of their own employees. Members of Serpentis Corp deemed too addicted to their own products are sent to Serpentis prison facilities. Here the inmates are subjected to extreme detoxing methods, from complete isolation to gene-alteration procedures. Rumors persist of Serpentis using the more “creative” of their inmates in extreme R and D programs, essentially working them to death in pursuit of better starship modules. DED Threat Assessment: 8 of 10"

The site can be difficult due to energy neutralizer sentries in the second room. Advise fitting for a strong tank against EM and Thermal damage. Warping to the site lands you on an ancient acceleration gate.

Should any CONCORD or otherwise friendly forces discover the location of this site, their standing orders are to engage and eliminate any enemy forces, then perform a follow-up strike on any further Sansha bases revealed through gathered intel.

Energy Meutralizer Sentries have a decent amount of armor, so it takes a while to destroy them, even with a high DPS battleship. Their range is exactly 115km. If you are in range of both Energy Neutralizer Sentries and don't have a cap booster fitted, you will run out of capacitor before you destroy even one of them. Some good approaches are either using cap boosters or a Micro Jump Drive to get out of the range of the sentries. It is not mandatory to destroy them for the site to be completed and/or to get an escalation.

sections.Walkthrough:
Warpin message:


"The Maze" is the name of this mysterious Gurista military complex. Most of those who have dared venture inside have not lived to tell about it, and those who are still alive are now labour workers for the Gurista.
Although never confirmed, the deepest pockets of this complex are rumored to be the home of Fatal himself.


The maze contains a large number of acceleration gates. In each room one gate takes you deeper into the complex while the rest of them lead back to the first pocket.


1st pocket
The gate is unlocked and the rats do not need to be killed. But you will most likely have to fly through this pocket many times so thinning out the rats is recommended.
In the room there are twelve acceleration gates. One of them leads deeper into the complex while the rest loop back. Take the bottom gate.
Experienced explorers will quickly establish a simple method to always choose the correct acceleration gate. Before taking any gate, drop a canister in front of it. If the gate returns you to the entrance, proceed to the next gate you wish to try and drop a canister in front of that one. Label each can and proceed to continue with this process until you find the right gate - make a note of which can is in front of the proper gate and either ignore or destroy all of the others.
Each acceleration gate will first send you to an empty room and then back to the beginning of the complex. When you activate the second acceleration gate, you will instantly be able to tell whether or not you are being returned to the beginning - if you are, the intro window bearing the complex's description will pop up once again.

Warning: The Fleet Outpost fires Kinetic Citadel Torpedoes that can instantly destroy any craft not suitably equipped for it! https://zkillboard.com/ship/25528/
This room contains a mixture of frigates, cruisers, and battleships along with cruise missile batteries and the fleet outpost (firing Citadel Kinetic Torpedoes). Upon attacking the outpost 4 battleships spawn. The outpost will release more waves of defenders as it takes damage. Upon the outpost's destruction a jetcan will appear with a 23rd Tier Overseer's Personal Effects and possibly additional loot including Pith-X Type modules or a Rattlesnake BPC. 
There are 4 additional spawns that occur at damage intervals on the station. A spawn will occur at half-shield, half-armor. two-thirds structure and one third structure. Once you have reduced the station to one third structure you may as well finish it off before dealing with the spawn. Database Entry for structure stats.

npcTables.text: Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel.

sections.Walkthrough:
On warpin:


Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel.

Warping to beacon for close range would require a very good tank to handle the swarm’s incoming damage. Using a propulsion module or a Micro Jump Drive is recommended. Single ungated normal space pocket.

sections.Escalation:
This anomaly can escalate to Angel Repurposed Outpost.



On warp in:


To the frustration of the Minmatar Republic, the Angel Cartel still enjoys a great deal of success with piracy throughout Republic space. A contributing factor to the Angel's ubiquity is their ability to repurpose small gang outposts as camps.
A Domination Grigori is sent with a small frigate wing to clear out a minor smuggler outpost. Once temporary fortifications are made, the location is further masked with a series of interference broadcasts designed to confound Republic sensors.
DED Threat Assessment: Unsafe (3 of 10)

npcTables.text: "Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel."

sections.Walkthrough:
Ungated normal space pocket. There are 2 variants of this anomaly, commonly referred to as "gas" or "rock" haven by players. The rock haven variant is readily identified by the warp-in popup received. Each also feature different structures within the site.
Note: In Case of a Dreadnought spawn, note that if the only Ship left is the Dreadnought you must not leave the Site or the Dreadnought will despawn!

sections.Walktrhough:
The site is an open Deadspace pocket, one may warp to preferred distance. With the initial group and 4 triggered spawns, there is a total of 79 ships. Warning: Spawns on this variant of anomaly are much bigger, incoming DPS can become overwhelming if triggers are blown up too early.

On warp in:


Operating from the heart of the Curse region, the Angel Cartel is today the largest and best organized of the space-based criminal factions. The Angels are divided into several groups, each with a very special function; it is commanded by the Dominations. In the century they've been lurking in deep space, they have stolen, plundered, or sabotaged countless number of ships and kidnapped, assaulted, or murdered thousands of people. The Angels recruit members from all the races, and are thus not bound to any one zone of operation; instead, their reach spans almost the entire known universe. Many believe that the Angels got their power by uncovering Jovian technologies hidden in their ancient homeworlds, now infested by the Angel Cartel.
